# CI Rescue

> Fixed-scope GitHub Actions diagnosis and repair with public proof artifacts.

Canonical site: https://yunczo.github.io/ci-rescue-service/
Repository: https://github.com/Yunczo/ci-rescue-service
No-account encrypted intake: https://yunczo.github.io/ci-rescue-service/#anonymous-intake
Public GitHub intake: https://github.com/Yunczo/ci-rescue-service/issues/new?template=service-request.yml

## Service

- Price: US$49 equivalent in Bitcoin, quoted in writing before payment.
- Scope: one GitHub Actions workflow and either one failing job path or one defined proactive Node 24 migration goal.
- Response: normally within 24 hours.
- Delivery: within 48 hours after confirmed payment and sanitized inputs.
- Deliverable: focused diagnosis, reviewable patch or unified diff, verification report, and one in-scope revision.
- Excluded: security or vulnerability work, credentials, production access, private data in public issues, and full CI/CD redesigns.
- Intake: browser-local NIP-17 ticket or public GitHub issue; never send secrets or private data.

## Public proof

- Runnable proof workflow: https://github.com/Yunczo/ci-rescue-service/actions/workflows/public-proof.yml
- Free offline GitHub Action: https://github.com/Yunczo/ci-rescue-diagnostic-action
- Free MIT-licensed toolkit: https://github.com/Yunczo/ci-rescue-service/releases/tag/toolkit-v1.0.0
- Release verification: https://github.com/Yunczo/ci-rescue-service/blob/main/RELEASE_VERIFICATION.md
- Synthetic example: https://github.com/Yunczo/ci-rescue-service/blob/main/examples/before-after.md

The examples are synthetic and are not represented as paid-client results.

## Free guides

- npm ci lockfile errors: https://yunczo.github.io/ci-rescue-service/guides/npm-ci-lockfile-github-actions.html
- package-lock lookup in monorepos: https://yunczo.github.io/ci-rescue-service/guides/package-lock-monorepo-github-actions.html
- upload-artifact missing files: https://yunczo.github.io/ci-rescue-service/guides/upload-artifact-no-files-found-github-actions.html
- pytest exit code 5 / no tests collected: https://yunczo.github.io/ci-rescue-service/guides/pytest-exit-code-5-no-tests-github-actions.html
- GitHub Actions Node 24 migration checklist: https://yunczo.github.io/ci-rescue-service/guides/github-actions-node24-migration.html
- Synthetic GitHub Actions Node 24 migration proof: https://yunczo.github.io/ci-rescue-service/guides/github-actions-node24-migration-proof.html

## Free browser tool

- Local GitHub Actions log triage: https://yunczo.github.io/ci-rescue-service/tools/github-actions-log-triage.html
- Local GitHub Actions Node 24 workflow checker: https://yunczo.github.io/ci-rescue-service/tools/github-actions-node24-checker.html

Both browser tools analyze sanitized input locally. Their Content Security Policies block browser connection APIs, and they contain no tracking or persistence code. The log analyzer reports line numbers rather than raw log text. The GitHub.com Node 24 checker uses dated Node 24 release floors and latest-release snapshots, and routes pinned SHAs, local or third-party actions, and ambiguous refs to manual review rather than guessing. The guide documents the separate GitHub Enterprise Server artifact path. Users must still sanitize inputs because browser extensions and the user's device are outside the page boundary.

## Payment boundaries

Service buyers must not pay before an intake reply confirms scope, exact BTC amount, address, quote expiry, and delivery window. Voluntary toolkit support is separate from the service and creates no delivery or priority obligation.
